Outlaws Motorcycle Club.description: The Outlaws Motorcycle Club, also known as the American Outlaws Association, is an international motorcycle gang founded in 1935 in McCook, Illinois. Classified as an organized crime syndicate by law enforcement, it is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and violent acts. The club has over 5,000 members across 441 chapters in 43 countries and is still active.

Clan del Golfo.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary and terrorist organization that emerged after the demobilization of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC) between 2003 and 2006. It is considered the largest, most dangerous, and best-structured criminal group in Colombia, involved in drug trafficking, massacres, extortion, kidnapping, and other crimes. The group operates in hundreds of municipalities across Colombia and has an estimated 9,000 to 14,000 members. Its former leader Dairo Antonio Úsuga David (alias Otoniel) was captured in October 2021.

Sarasota Assassination Society.description: A late 19th-century secret organization in Manatee County, Florida, founded in 1884 by Alfred Bidwell, Leonard Andrews, and Jason Alford. Composed of 20-22 former Confederate soldiers, it engaged in murder of political opponents, blackmail, and political intimidation. The group was responsible for the murders of Charles E. Abbe and Harrison T. Riley, leading to indictments and trials of its members. It was active from 1884 to 1892.

Hamas.description: Hamas is a Sunni Islamist Palestinian nationalist political organization with a military wing, the al-Qassam Brigades. Founded in 1987 by Ahmed Yassin during the First Intifada, it has governed the Gaza Strip since 2007. It advocates for Palestinian statehood and resistance against Israeli occupation, and has been involved in multiple conflicts with Israel.

19th Street Gang.description: The 19th Street Gang was a New York City predominantly Irish street gang during the 1870s, known for anti-Protestant violence. Led by Little Mike, the gang consisted of young pickpockets, muggers, and sneak thieves operating around 19th to 34th Street. They targeted defenseless victims but sometimes spared local Catholics. The gang disappeared by the end of the 1880s.
